title: orchis traunsteineri subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Orchis traunsteineri subsp. ochroleuca, also known as the yellow-green man orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid found in the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Orchis traunsteineri subsp. ochroleuca include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
